title: Information Theory — SuperBEST v4 Cost Analysis
key_insight: Information theory is built on p * ln(p) — ln costs 1n, mul costs 2n. The critical question per equation is whether terms are sign-definite (unlocking add_pos=3n) or can be individually negative (requiring add_gen=11n). This single sign question is the primary cost driver across the entire domain.

sign_analysis.verdict: Restructure as sum of -p_i*ln(p_i): each term non-negative => add_pos=3n

breakdown.strategy: Compute neg(ln(p_i)) first, then mul, yielding positive terms. Sum with add_pos.

rejected_strategy.description: Compute p_i*ln(p_i) directly (negative terms), sum with add_gen, then negate
rejected_strategy.why_worse: 14N-9 vs 8N-3: add_gen (11n) vs add_pos (3n) for N-1 additions

equations.name: KL Divergence
equations.domain: Information theory, Bayesian inference, variational methods

sign_analysis.single_term_sign: p_i*ln(p_i/q_i) can be positive (p_i > q_i) or negative (p_i < q_i)
sign_analysis.global_sum_sign: D_KL >= 0 (Gibbs inequality), but individual terms are mixed sign
sign_analysis.verdict: Cannot use add_pos at term level — must use add_gen=11n for all N-1 additions

equations.cost_vs_shannon: Exactly 2x Shannon minus constant: (16N-11) vs (8N-3). The doubling comes entirely from add_gen (11n) replacing add_pos (3n), costing 8 extra per addition.

equations.name: Cross-Entropy Loss
equations.domain: ML training loss, information theory, maximum likelihood

sign_analysis.verdict: Each term p_i * (-ln q_i) >= 0 — same structure as Shannon entropy => add_pos

breakdown.additions: (N-1) * add_pos = 3(N-1)  [all terms non-negative, no outer neg needed]

equations.structural_isomorphism: H(P,Q) and H(P,P)=H(P) have identical EML cost (8N-3). The ML cross-entropy loss is the same formula as Shannon entropy with the predicted distribution substituted for the true distribution. Zero cost difference.

equations.name: Mutual Information
equations.domain: Information theory, feature selection, causal inference
equations.n_dependence: O(|X|*|Y|), double sum over joint distribution

sign_analysis.single_term_sign: p(x,y)*ln(p(x,y)/(p(x)p(y))) can be positive or negative individually
sign_analysis.global_sum_sign: I(X;Y) >= 0, but individual cells are mixed sign

equations.name: Perplexity
equations.n_dependence: O(N), N = sequence length or vocabulary

sign_analysis.note: Sum of same-sign (negative) values still requires add_gen — sign uniformity alone is insufficient; add_pos requires positivity

steps.note: add_gen: values are negative, not positive
negate the negative sum to get positive

equations.cost_insight: Perplexity costs 12N-6 vs Shannon's 8N-3 for N terms. The 4N-3 overhead comes from: (a) losing add_pos (negative ln values force add_gen, costing 8n extra per addition), and (b) the additional neg+div+exp steps. The add_gen dominance is the key bottleneck.

equations.name: Entropy Rate (Markov Chain)
equations.domain: Information theory, stochastic processes, ergodic theory

sign_analysis.inner_H_i: Shannon entropy of row i — each inner term is non-negative

inner_loop.description: For each state i, compute H_i = Shannon entropy of transition row P_ij

equations.name: Conditional Entropy
equations.domain: Information theory, channel capacity, decision trees

sign_analysis.verdict: All |X||Y| terms are non-negative => add_pos for full sum

equations.comparison_with_mi: Conditional entropy (10|X||Y|-3) is cheaper than mutual information (18|X||Y|-11) despite similar structure. The key difference: H(Y|X) terms are always non-negative (add_pos=3n), while I(X;Y) terms are mixed sign (add_gen=11n). This 8n-per-addition gap drives the cost difference.

equations.name: Jensen-Shannon Divergence
equations.domain: Information theory, distributional distance, generative models

step_1_mixture.description: Compute M_i = (p_i + q_i)/2 for each i

step_6_subtract.note: sub=2n always; result JSD >= 0 by convexity of entropy

equations.cost_insight: JSD = 3 Shannon evaluations + mixture computation. The 29N-2 cost vs Shannon's 8N-3 reflects the 3x structural overhead. JSD is bounded: 0 <= JSD <= ln(2), making it a superior distance metric to KL (unbounded) at 3.6x its cost.

isomorphisms.name: Shannon = Boltzmann entropy
isomorphisms.note: Identical formula; k_B just scales the result by a free constant. Zero EML cost difference.

isomorphisms.name: KL divergence = free energy difference
isomorphisms.note: KL divergence from equilibrium measures thermodynamic free energy cost of non-equilibrium distribution.

isomorphisms.name: Cross-entropy loss = Shannon cross-entropy
isomorphisms.note: Identical formula; ML training minimizes Shannon cross-entropy. Cost is the same whether viewed as ML or IT.

isomorphisms.name: Perplexity = partition function analog
isomorphisms.IT_form: PP = exp(-1/N * sum ln p_i) = exp(H)  [for uniform]
isomorphisms.note: Both are exponentials of sums of log-probabilities/energies. PP measures average surprise; Z measures accessible states.

isomorphisms.name: Mutual information = channel capacity core
isomorphisms.note: I(X;Y) is the per-distribution cost (18|X||Y|-11); optimization over input distribution adds additional structure.

rows: extra mul for marginals; add_gen (signed terms)
quadratic: inner Shannon loop per state
div for p(y|x) but terms positive => add_pos
3 Shannon evals + mixture; most expensive per-N
summary_table.note_N10: For equations 4, 6, 7: N=10 means |X|=|Y|=10 (100 cells/pairs) or |S|=10 states

eml_domain_assessment.verdict: Information theory is the most EML-native applied domain after pure mathematics
rationale: Core primitive is p*ln(p): ln=1n + mul=2n = 3n per weighted log term
Shannon entropy 8N-3 is among the lowest costs for any N-term statistical formula
Cross-entropy ML loss is structurally identical to IT entropy: zero overhead for ML training
The dominant cost driver is sign structure, not computational complexity
Nearly all costs collapse to 8N-3 (positive terms) or 16N-11 (signed terms)
The 8n factor difference between add_pos and add_gen is the main architectural lever
eml_domain_assessment.design_principle: The central optimization question for any information-theoretic formula: are the summands sign-definite? If yes, 3n/addition; if no, 11n/addition. Restructuring formulas to expose positive terms is the primary cost reduction strategy.
